When running the tests on my 4GB GPU (NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050) I got a cuda out of memory.
I want to add a pull request to lower the GPU RAM requirement to 1GB to enable development&testing on low-cost GPUs.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8560","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8560/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8560/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8560/events","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8560","id":2485108014,"node_id":"I_kwDOBFKgkM6UH70u","number":8560,"title":"Implement cupy.ndarray == None and cupy.ndarray != None","user":{"login":"yonikremer","id":76044840,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2MDQ0ODQw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/76044840?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer","html_url":"https://github.com/yonikremer","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":1360743887,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzYwNzQzODg3","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/issue-checked","name":"issue-checked","color":"ffffff","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-08-25T07:43:41Z","updated_at":"2024-09-04T11:42:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

In numpy, you can use `==` and `!=` to check which item in an `ndarray` is None.
In cupy, comparing an array to None raises `NotImplemented`.

Numpy example:
```
import numpy as np
>>> a = np.array([1, 2, None])
>>> a == None
array([False, False,  True])
```

Cupy example:
```
import cupy
>>> a = cupy.array([1, 2, cupy.nan])
>>> a == None
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "cupy/_core/core.pyx", line 1217, in cupy._core.core._ndarray_base.__richcmp__
TypeError: operand type(s) all returned NotImplemented from __array_ufunc__(<ufunc 'equal'>, '__call__', array([ 1.,  2., nan]), None): 'ndarray', 'NoneType'
```

Notice that numpy checks equality against `None` and not against `np.nan`

### Additional Information

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/7569","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/7569/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/7569/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/7569/events","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/7569","id":1712764385,"node_id":"I_kwDOBFKgkM5mFrXh","number":7569,"title":"Implementing numpy.ndarray.itemset","user":{"login":"rwschonberg","id":47501218,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ3NTAxMjE4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/47501218?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/rwschonberg","html_url":"https://github.com/rwschonberg","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rwschonberg/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rwschonberg/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rwschonberg/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rwschonberg/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rwschonberg/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rwschonberg/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rwschonberg/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rwschonberg/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rwschonberg/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":545923268,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw1NDU5MjMyNjg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/cat:feature","name":"cat:feature","color":"fbca04","default":false,"description":"New features/APIs"},{"id":1052989469,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDUyOTg5NDY5","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/prio:medium","name":"prio:medium","color":"c686e0","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2023-05-16T21:31:21Z","updated_at":"2024-09-07T11:08:26Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

Hi all - first-time contributor here, so I went to https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/6078#issue-1051191869 and picked an API under the Easy category to implement: `numpy.ndarray.itemset`. In line with the recommendation in the [Contribution Guide](https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/contribution.html#issues-and-pull-requests) to submit an issue for new APIs, I'm creating this post to discuss the design.

tl;dr - Two questions: should the code be contained in `cupy._core.core.pyx`? Since it's modifying on-device arrays, should it have its own kernel or make use of others that could do the job?

I found the TODO that (in theory) marks where the function should go in `cupy._core.core.pyx`. Looking at other similar functions there which modify data on the GPU, it seems there are two standard implementations. The first is to expose a method in `_ndarray_base` which calls a kernel (e.g. `_ndarray_base.fill`). The second is to expose a method in `_ndarray_base` which calls to another method in a different `_routines_*.pyx` file. **Organizationally speaking, is there another `.pyx` file where the private implementation of `ndarray.itemset` should live**? It's a simple enough function that I think it would make sense for it to just live in `core.pyx`, but I'm not sure what the precedent is.

Looking at the expected behavior of `numpy.ndarray.itemset`, it seems like the only mode of operation is modifying a singular value in the ndarray. This brings me to my next question - for this simple of a function, **is a dedicated kernel necessary or can I make use of another pre-existing kernel?** There are a few other methods that would already provide the abstraction of "set some elements in an on-device array to some value," so that could potentially also work for `ndarray.itemset`.

### Additional Information

that ufuncs make different type promotion between numpy and cupy when the argument is a zero-dim array. See #7295. @pri1311 found this issue. Thanks!

```py
>>> x_np = np.array([4]).astype(np.float32)
>>> y1_np = np.array([2])  # int64
>>> y2_np = np.array(2)    # int64
>>> y3_np = 2
>>> x_np / y1_np
array([2.])
>>> x_np / y2_np
array([2.], dtype=float32)
>>> x_np / y3_np
array([2.], dtype=float32)
```
```py
>>> x_cp = cp.array([4]).astype(cp.float32)
>>> y1_cp = cp.array([2])  # int64
>>> y2_cp = cp.array(2)    # int64
>>> y3_cp = 2
>>> x_cp / y1_cp
array([2.])
>>> x_cp / y2_cp
array([2.])                # !!!
>>> x_cp / y3_cp
array([2.], dtype=float32)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/7418/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/7418/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/comments/2335154759","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/7418#issuecomment-2335154759","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/7418","id":2335154759,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBFKgkM6LL6JH","user":{"login":"yonikremer","id":76044840,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2MDQ0ODQw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/76044840?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer","html_url":"https://github.com/yonikremer","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-07T11:11:15Z","updated_at":"2024-09-07T11:11:15Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"@takagi Can you close the issue?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/comments/2335154759/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

issue","color":"49890C","default":true,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":21,"created_at":"2021-11-11T17:11:31Z","updated_at":"2024-09-12T04:23:08Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Implement GPU version of `numpy.*` functions in `cupy.*` namespace.

This is a tracker issue that lists the remaining `numpy.*` APIs (see also: [comparison table](https://docs.cupy.dev/en/latest/reference/comparison.html#module-level)). I've categorized them based on difficulty so that new contributors can pick the right task. Your contribution is highly welcomed and appreciated!

## List of APIs

```[tasklist]
### Very Easy
- [x] `numpy.asfarray` (#6085)
- [x] `numpy.byte_bounds` (hint: `ndarray.data.ptr`) (#7015)
- [x] `numpy.format_float_positional` (#6308)
- [x] `numpy.format_float_scientific` (#6474)
- [x] `numpy.ndarray.searchsorted` (#7059)
```

```[tasklist]
### Easy
- [ ] `numpy.apply_over_axes`
- [x] `numpy.array_equiv` (#6254)
- [x] `numpy.asarray_chkfinite` (#6275)
- [x] `numpy.fabs` (#6282)
- [x] `numpy.float_power` (#6371)
- [x] `numpy.heaviside` (#6798)
- [x] `numpy.isneginf` (#6089)
- [x] `numpy.isposinf` (#6089)
- [x] `numpy.mask_indices` (#6156)
- [ ] `numpy.ndarray.itemset` (#7459)
- [x] `numpy.real_if_close` (#6475)
- [x] `numpy.setdiff1d` (#6433)
- [x] `numpy.setxor1d` (#6582)
- [x] `numpy.tril_indices` & `numpy.tril_indices_from` (#6305)
- [x] `numpy.triu_indices` & `numpy.triu_indices_from` (#6316)
- [x] `numpy.union1d` (#6357)
- [ ] `numpy.lib.format.*`
- [ ] `numpy.lib.stride_tricks.sliding_window_view` (#6956, #7575)
- [ ] `numpy.emath.*` (`numpy.lib.scimath.*`) (#7295)
```

```[tasklist]
### Medium
- [ ] `numpy.block`
- [x] `numpy.delete` (#7359)
- [ ] `numpy.geomspace`
- [ ] `numpy.insert` (#6597)
- [ ] `numpy.put_along_axis`
- [x] `numpy.row_stack` (#6312)
- [ ] `numpy.spacing`
- [x] `numpy.vander` (#6279)
```

```[tasklist]
### Medium to Hard
- [ ] `numpy.histogram_bin_edges`
- [x] `numpy.ediff1d` (#6280)
- [x] `numpy.intersect1d` (#6402, #6407)
- [ ] `numpy.nanpercentile`
- [ ] `numpy.nanquantile`
- [ ] `numpy.ndarray.ctypes` (note: needs design discussion)

- [ ] `numpy.polynomial.*`

- [ ] `numpy.polyder` (#6469)
- [ ] `numpy.polydiv` (#3780)
- [ ] `numpy.polyint`
- [x] `numpy.trapz` (#6107)
- [ ] `numpy.linalg.cond`
- [ ] `numpy.linalg.eig`
- [ ] `numpy.linalg.eigvals`
- [ ] `numpy.linalg.multi_dot` (#6358)
- [ ] `numpy.random.Generator.*` (see the dedicated tracker issue for details: https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/4557)
```

```[tasklist]
### Low priority (iterator functions)
- [ ] `numpy.ndenumerate`
- [ ] `numpy.nditer`
- [ ] `numpy.nested_iters`
```

```[tasklist]
### Low priority (help functions)
- [ ] `numpy.info`
- [ ] `numpy.lookfor`
- [ ] `numpy.source`
```

```[tasklist]
### Low priority (internal functions)
- [ ] `numpy.deprecate`
- [ ] `numpy.deprecate_with_doc`
```

```[tasklist]
### Low priority (dtype APIs - need to filter types unsupported by CuPy)
- [ ] `numpy.maximum_sctype`
- [ ] `numpy.cast` (undocumented API) (hint: see `numpy/core/numerictypes.py`)
- [ ] `numpy.typecodes` (undocumented API)
- [ ] `numpy.sctypeDict` (undocumented API)
- [ ] `numpy.sctypes` (undocumented API)
- [ ] `numpy.nbytes` (undocumented API)
```

```[tasklist]
### Low priority (rarely used APIs)
- [ ] `numpy.einsum_path` (#6723)
- [ ] `numpy.frompyfunc` (maybe just call GUFunc?)
```

## Steps to Contribute

1. Fork and star :star: the CuPy repository :wink:

1. Pick a function you want to work on. You can find the function in the [NumPy API Reference](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.html) to understand what should be implemented.

1. Implement a function in your branch. If you need help, join [Gitter](https://gitter.im/cupy/community) or just ask for help in this issue.

1. Don't forget to write a test code!

1. Build CuPy and run tests to confirm that the function runs fine:
  `pip install -e . && pytest tests/cupy_tests/PATH_TO_YOUR_TEST`
   See the [Contribution Guide](https://docs.cupy.dev/en/latest/contribution.html#unit-testing) for details.

1. Submit a pull-request to the `main` branch.

Note that you will need a GPU environment to develop CuPy.

I've categorized them based on difficulty so that new contributors can pick the right task. Your contribution is highly welcomed and appreciated!

ufunc makes different type promotion from numpy does when a zero-dim array is supplied","user":{"login":"takagi","id":1224375,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyMjQzNzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1224375?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/takagi","html_url":"https://github.com/takagi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/takagi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/takagi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/takagi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/takagi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/takagi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/takagi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/takagi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/takagi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/takagi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":1360743887,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzYwNzQzODg3","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/issue-checked","name":"issue-checked","color":"ffffff","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2023-02-21T06:47:18Z","updated_at":"2024-08-23T07:29:27Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Supposed that ufuncs make different type promotion between numpy and cupy when the argument is a zero-dim array. See #7295. @pri1311 found this issue. Thanks!

```py
>>> x_np = np.array([4]).astype(np.float32)
>>> y1_np = np.array([2])  # int64
>>> y2_np = np.array(2)    # int64
>>> y3_np = 2
>>> x_np / y1_np
array([2.])
>>> x_np / y2_np
array([2.], dtype=float32)
>>> x_np / y3_np
array([2.], dtype=float32)
```
```py
>>> x_cp = cp.array([4]).astype(cp.float32)
>>> y1_cp = cp.array([2])  # int64
>>> y2_cp = cp.array(2)    # int64
>>> y3_cp = 2
>>> x_cp / y1_cp
array([2.])
>>> x_cp / y2_cp
array([2.])                # !!!
>>> x_cp / y3_cp
array([2.], dtype=float32)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/7418/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/7418/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/comments/2306468489","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/7418#issuecomment-2306468489","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/7418","id":2306468489,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBFKgkM6JeeqJ","user":{"login":"yonikremer","id":76044840,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2MDQ0ODQw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/76044840?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer","html_url":"https://github.com/yonikremer","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-23T07:29:27Z","updated_at":"2024-08-23T07:29:27Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"In numpy 2.1, I get:
```
>>> x_np = np.array([4]).astype(np.float32)
>>> y1_np = np.array([2])  # int64
>>> y2_np = np.array(2)    # int64
>>> y3_np = 2
>>> x_np / y1_np
array([2.])  # float64
>>> x_np / y2_np
array([2.])  # float64
>>> x_np / y3_np
array([2.], dtype=float32)
```

It seems like numpy fixed their issue.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/comments/2306468489/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8560","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8560/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8560/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8560/events","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8560","id":2485108014,"node_id":"I_kwDOBFKgkM6UH70u","number":8560,"title":"Implement cupy.ndarray == None and cupy.ndarray != None","user":{"login":"yonikremer","id":76044840,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2MDQ0ODQw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/76044840?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer","html_url":"https://github.com/yonikremer","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/yonikremer/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":545923268,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw1NDU5MjMyNjg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/cat:feature","name":"cat:feature","color":"fbca04","default":false,"description":"New features/APIs"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-08-25T07:43:41Z","updated_at":"2024-08-25T07:43:41Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

In numpy, you can use `==` and `!=` to check which item in an `ndarray` is None.
In cupy, comparing an array to None raises `NotImplemented`.

Numpy example:
```
import numpy as np
>>> a = np.array([1, 2, None])
>>> a == None
array([False, False,  True])
```

Cupy example:
```
import cupy
>>> a = cupy.array([1, 2, cupy.nan])
>>> a == None
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "cupy/_core/core.pyx", line 1217, in cupy._core.core._ndarray_base.__richcmp__
TypeError: operand type(s) all returned NotImplemented from __array_ufunc__(<ufunc 'equal'>, '__call__', array([ 1.,  2., nan]), None): 'ndarray', 'NoneType'
```

Notice that numpy checks equality against `None` and not against `np.nan`

In numpy, you can use `==` and `!=` to check which item in an `ndarray` is None.
In cupy, comparing an array to None raises `NotImplemented`.

Numpy example:
```
import numpy as np
>>> a = np.array([1, 2, None])
>>> a == None
array([False, False,  True])
```

Cupy example:
```
import cupy
>>> a = cupy.array([1, 2, cupy.nan])
>>> a == None
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "cupy/_core/core.pyx", line 1217, in cupy._core.core._ndarray_base.__richcmp__
TypeError: operand type(s) all returned NotImplemented from __array_ufunc__(<ufunc 'equal'>, '__call__', array([ 1.,  2., nan]), None): 'ndarray', 'NoneType'
```

Notice that numpy checks equality against `None` and not against `np.nan`

When running the tests on my 4GB GPU (NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050) I got a cuda out of memory.
I want to add a pull request to lower the GPU RAM requirement to 1GB to enable development&testing on low-cost GPUs.
